title: CLI /! command: no size limit on command output before MLS encryption
description: commands.rs /! handler truncates stdout at 4096 bytes before display but then calls mls.encrypt(&line_bytes) where line_bytes is the full serde_json-wrapped ClearMessage. The truncation happens to 'display' (the string formatted for output), not to 'line_bytes'. The code: let display = if truncated { format!(...truncated...) } else { text.trim_end() }; then let line_bytes = serde_json::to_vec(&ClearMessage::Chat { text: display }). The truncated flag is set from stdout.len() > 4096, and stdout is truncated before conversion to UTF-8 lossy. So line_bytes contains at most ~4096 bytes of content plus JSON overhead. This is fine — correctly bounded. However the current code is confusing: 'truncated' is computed on the raw bytes, 'display' is the (possibly truncated) string, and 'line_bytes' uses 'display'. A future contributor might accidentally use 'text' (the untruncated string) instead of 'display'. Flag as clarity/maintainability risk.
close_reason: CLI /! already truncates stdout to 4096 bytes before MLS encrypt
